items.caption: Best Beef Rendang ive ever had @Munchie Peachy  📍Samara Kitchen @Samara Kitchen  #indonesianfood #rendang #Foodie #eating #hiddengems #supportsmallbusiness
items.transcript: [00:00-00:07] My wife and I were craving Indonesian food, and we heard that this small Indonesian restaurant in this industrial complex served some of the best Indonesian food. [00:07-00:09] We didn't know what to get, so we asked the chef. [00:09-00:10] Hey, man. [00:10-00:11] Hello, hello. [00:11-00:14] If you could, like, get us a bunch of your favorite dishes. [00:14-00:14] Okay. [00:14-00:15] Did you be able to do that? [00:15-00:16] Okay. [00:16-00:16] That was it. [00:16-00:19] The owner starred us off with his signature lamb skewers. [00:19-00:22] Immediately, I loved the syrup on those lamb skewers. [00:22-00:24] It was sweet and savory, and I loved that peanut sauce. [00:24-00:28] But what surprised me the most were these rice cakes dipped in peanut sauce. [00:28-00:30] They taste like potatoes, but with a mochi texture. [00:30-00:34] He then served this as gigantic meatball called Baksa Rudol. [00:34-00:36] The meatball was the size of my face. [00:36-00:39] It reminded me of the Vietnamese meatball, but in gigantic form. [00:39-00:42] But the owner told us that this is his number one most ordered dish. [00:42-00:44] Nasi Randang, and it blew my mind away. [00:44-00:46] So freaking tender. [00:46-00:47] I'm not gonna even use my teeth. [00:47-00:49] Can you give us a show, guys? [00:51-00:53] I learned I did not use my teeth. [00:53-00:53] I swear. [00:53-00:56] This is like, so tender, so flavorful. [00:56-00:59] This might be the best beef randang I've ever eaten in my life.

items.address: 795 St Clair Ave W, Toronto, ON M6C 1B9, Canada
items.caption: Only eating at Palestinian Restaurants for a full day with @Munchie Peachy 1) @FlamingStove Palestinian Style Shawarma 2) Louf Palestinian Food (invited) 3) @Kunafas  #palestinianfood #palestinian #kunafa #shawarma #eating
items.transcript: [00:00-00:04] Today I'm only eating a Palestinian restaurant, starting with Palestinian-style shawarma, [00:04-00:06] hidden away in the back of this hasty market. [00:06-00:09] But I'm curious, what is Palestinian-style shawarma? [00:09-00:11] The owner told me it all starts with the bread. [00:11-00:16] It's called saj bread, and it's a very thin bread that they make fresh daily, completely from scratch. [00:16-00:19] They put the bread on this dough, and it starts bubbling and cooking within seconds. [00:19-00:21] But the chicken shawarma meat is also very special. [00:21-00:25] It's marinated in Palestinian spices and yogurt for three days, [00:25-00:27] and the key move for me is this ambasauce. [00:27-00:31] It's a tangy, mango-y, vibrant sauce that goes into Palestinian-style shawarma. [00:31-00:36] But these shawarma are so big that even after cutting in a half, it looks like you get two full shawarma. [00:36-00:38] But I've never had a shawarma that tastes like this. [00:38-00:40] The chicken meat is so well seasoned from the spices. [00:40-00:43] The ambasauce gives it an incredible, vibrant kick. [00:43-00:45] This might be my favorite shawarma of all time. [00:45-00:47] For dinner, we went to this fine dining Palestinian restaurant. [00:47-00:50] You walk up, and it looks like someone's home that you're entering. [00:50-00:53] And this was one of the most meaningful dining experiences for us in 2025. [00:53-00:55] Everything in this restaurant speaks of the Palestinian people, [00:55-00:57] their identity and culture. [00:57-00:59] The plates are handcrafted in Palestine. [00:59-01:00] The wine comes from Upper Galilee. [01:00-01:02] It was such a special experience for me to try this wine. [01:02-01:05] Many of the ingredients also come from Palestine, like the olives, [01:05-01:07] and the olive oil used in many of the dishes. [01:07-01:10] My favorite dish was this lamb tenderloin bests-thirma. [01:10-01:13] The lamb is so tender, and the sauce was so frequent, so aromatic. [01:13-01:16] But for me, the most special dish here is their canoffas. [01:16-01:20] It's a canoffa that the owner and chef grew up eating in his hometown of Bethlehem. [01:20-01:22] It's so different from any canoffa I've ever had. [01:22-01:24] And that's why for dessert, I went back to my favorite canoffa shawarma. [01:24-01:26] To try another style of Palestinian canoffa, [01:26-01:29] they make your canoffas fresh every 30 minutes. [01:29-01:32] And they use a very special cheese that is imported from Palestine. [01:32-01:35] It's cheesy, surrepes, so satisfying, especially fresh. [01:35-01:36] Still my favorite canoffa of all time.

items.address: 1210 Kennedy Rd, Scarborough, ON M1P 2L1, Canada
items.caption: My friend planned an Indian Food Crawl for me #indianfood  📍Federick (Indian Chinese Hakka) 📍 King Of Punjabi Tadka  📍 Hyderabad Biryani Hut 📍 Kairali (Kerala cuisine)
items.transcript: [00:00-00:05] Trying Indian food I've never had for the first time my friend has been planning for months to prepare this Indian food [00:05-00:09] And I had no idea India had so many different cuisines first that we went to the iconic [00:09-00:13] Fredrick's for some Indochinese Haka cuisine got the Haka [00:13-00:17] Chow Mean and their legendary chili chicken. I love how gigantic the portions are and it only costs [00:18-00:23] 975 and it comes with a drink the owner is originally from Kolkata where Indochinese cuisine is popular next up [00:23-00:25] We drove to an industrial complex to try butter chicken [00:25-00:28] I had butter chicken before but this one had a thicker gravy texture [00:28-00:32] Which I kind of like and apparently if you go on a Thursday it only costs 899 [00:32-00:36] But we drove all the way to another plaza to try hydro baddie Briarani apparently what makes this [00:36-00:41] Briarani so special is how they cook the rice the rice is steamed with the chicken so all the chicken juices get [00:41-00:42] Absorbed by the rice [00:42-00:47] This is my first time trying this type of Briarani and I love the fragrance of the rice before our last stop [00:47-00:51] We're trying Kerala Indian cuisine Kerala is a state in India next to the ocean [00:51-00:55] So they're famous for their fish dishes. We got their kingfish curry combo which comes with a ton of side dishes [00:55-00:59] My friends family is actually from Kerala and this reminded him of the foodie a growing up

items.address: 36 Simcoe St N, Oshawa, ON L1G 4S1, Canada
items.caption: Butter Chicken Mexican Poutine ⬇️ I wanted to bring @Aashim Eats to @The Bollywood Tacos 🌮🌯 to try their curry lamb tacos. But we saw so many people ordering the butter chicken poutine that we had to try it as well! Game changing poutine!  #butterchicken #poutine #fusionfood #Foodie #eating
items.transcript: [00:00-00:02] Butter chicken, Mexican Coutine. [00:02-00:06] This might be the most insanely comforting Coutine I've ever eaten. [00:06-00:09] We drove all the way to Oshawa to just to try this Indian Mexican fusion. [00:09-00:14] You walk in and there's these huge cauldrons of butter chicken and curry chicken. [00:14-00:15] Slow cooking for hours. [00:15-00:17] And this is where the magic happens. [00:17-00:22] The Coutine is drowned in that delicious butter chicken sauce and slathered with Bollywood taco sauce. [00:22-00:25] When we asked the owner how he came up with these inventions, [00:25-00:30] he simply shared that he loved Mexican food and wanted to combine it with the food he enjoyed growing up eating. [00:30-00:32] We started with the curry chicken tistados. [00:32-00:34] That curry chicken combined with those Mexican toppings. [00:34-00:38] It was a flavor bomb, but I wanted Ashem to try my favorite taco here. [00:38-00:39] The curry lamb taco. [00:39-00:42] And he loved that mint chutney curry lamb combo. [00:42-00:45] But this is the dish we drove all the way to Oshawa for. [00:45-00:46] The butter chicken poutine. [00:46-00:50] This is Indian Mexican and Canadian comfort food all in one dish. [00:50-00:53] The butter chicken melts and fuses with the poutine cheese. [00:53-00:54] So comforting, so much flavor.

items.caption: The Halo Halo at the end 🔥 trying to spend $100 on food in Toronto Asian Night Market @Munchie Peachy
items.transcript: [00:00-00:05] Trying to spend $100 on food at an Asian Night Market here in Toronto and buckle up, this one's not gonna be cheap. [00:05-00:08] First up, we spent $10 on these gigantic lamb skewers. [00:08-00:14] I let my wife know how good they were through my dancing, but these might be some of the biggest taco yaki balls I've ever tried. [00:14-00:17] You get a baby octopus in each one, not just a little lid. [00:17-00:20] They were $15 for four, a bit on the pricier side. [00:20-00:22] They're made fresh to order exemplified by me burning myself. [00:22-00:24] But this stall is the absolute highlight. [00:24-00:29] Do you see pan-fried pork bun dumplings made fresh to order right before your eyes? [00:29-00:30] They cost $10 for three hefty ones. [00:30-00:33] Love the crispy bottom, love how juicy it was. [00:33-00:36] Support filling was extremely tender, and it got me laughing with joy. [00:36-00:38] But that's when I thought it couldn't get any better. [00:38-00:39] It got better. [00:39-00:41] For $10, the same stall sells these beef pies. [00:41-00:44] It's crispy on the outside and tender on the inside. [00:44-00:47] We've already spent $45, but it wouldn't be a proper date without dessert. [00:47-00:52] So for $10, you bought a mango mochi, and this is the number one dessert my wife and I wanted to try. [00:52-00:56] It's a Filipino dessert called HALO HALO, and I love how it comes with Ube ice cream.

items.address: 14 Market St., Toronto, ON M5E 1M6, Canada
items.caption: For my dad’s birthday I travelled over 14hrs to catch and cook him fresh seafood #catchandcook #prawns #eating #foodies #vancouverisland
items.transcript: [00:00-00:02] It's my dad's birthday and last year I bought him a king crab [00:02-00:04] But this year I'm gonna catch and cook him fresh seafood [00:04-00:07] I travel for over 14 hours to catch my dad's seafood [00:07-00:08] Flying from Toronto to Vancouver [00:08-00:11] Then taking a ferry with my uncle and my dad to Vancouver Island [00:11-00:14] Then driving another three hours to get to the other side of the island [00:14-00:16] Finally we're out on the ocean on my uncle's boat [00:16-00:17] He's been fishing here for decades [00:17-00:18] So he knows the best fishing spot [00:18-00:20] Immediately I caught a sea bass [00:20-00:21] My dad caught his favorite fish [00:21-00:22] A massive rock caught [00:22-00:24] These can go up to $50 a pound [00:24-00:25] They're very expensive [00:25-00:27] But we also caught another very expensive sea animal [00:27-00:29] The coveted BC spot prawns [00:29-00:31] These can also go for up to $50 a pound [00:31-00:33] We caught over a thousand dollars of seafood [00:33-00:35] For dinner we started with a spot prawns [00:35-00:36] They were still alive in kicking [00:36-00:38] We cooked them in boiling water for a minute and 10 seconds [00:38-00:41] And I think we ate a total of about 100 spot prawns [00:41-00:43] That's like 10 pounds of spot prawns [00:43-00:44] Or $500 a prawn [00:44-00:46] They were so juicy because of all the head butter [00:46-00:48] It was literally like eating butter [00:48-00:50] It was so creamy [00:50-00:52] And nothing beats live fresh spot prawns [00:52-00:53] You don't need anything else [00:53-00:55] The body is naturally sweet and muscular [00:55-00:57] We were live in the life drinking champagne eating prawns [00:57-00:59] But this red head row is my favorite part [00:59-01:00] It's like eating bougie yolk [01:00-01:02] After a hundred prawns we were full [01:02-01:03] But we still had to eat the rock caught [01:03-01:05] We cooked it into Cantonese way [01:05-01:07] And honestly this was the best rock caught I've ever had [01:07-01:09] But I think the best part was just spending time with my dad [01:09-01:10] And my uncle on his birthday

items.caption: Replying to @sydoprey this restaurant has been serving the community fish and chips for over 40 years @Standrewsfishnchips #fishandchips #supportlocalbusiness #cheapeats
items.transcript: [00:00-00:03] Hey Clement, my family owns a Fission Chips restaurant. [00:03-00:07] My grandfather opened it up when he immigrated from Scotland 40 years ago, [00:07-00:09] and now my mom owns it. [00:09-00:12] Thank you so much for sharing with us your family's Fission Chips restaurant. [00:12-00:15] Today, my wife and I are super excited to try it. [00:15-00:18] And one thing we notice right away is how hardworking the owner is. [00:18-00:21] She's the only one at the front of the restaurant, [00:21-00:24] and she's pretty much doing everything so we were really grateful for her service. [00:24-00:27] We started off with her signature Hattick Fission Chips. [00:27-00:29] It was the size of my arm. [00:29-00:32] This is just a small section of the Hattick and it's loaded with Hattick meat. [00:32-00:34] After my first bite, this is all I had to say. [00:34-00:37] They've been doing this single thing for 40 years. They perfected it. [00:37-00:40] Their fries are also made fresh in-house and combined with that delicious gravy, [00:40-00:42] it actually makes a really good poutine. [00:42-00:44] But this is my first time trying a meat pie. [00:44-00:47] I'm not sure if I'm supposed to pour the gravy on it like that, but I did it. [00:47-00:50] It's a very hearty meal, so make sure to prepare for a nap. [00:50-00:53] And for dessert, we decided to try their deep-fried jam sandwich. [00:53-00:56] It kind of reminded me of a raspberry pie with ice cream, [00:56-00:57] but with a savory deep-fried batter. [00:57-00:58] That's really good.

items.address: 20 Lebovic Ave #773, Scarborough, ON M1L 4V9, Canada
items.caption: $70 Filipino foodcourt feast at Seafood City Supermarket @SeafoodCityOfficial #filipinofood #cheapeats #seafoodcity #noodles
items.transcript: [00:00-00:04] This is everything we ate for $70 at Sea Food City's supermarket. [00:04-00:09] Bucko up because we're gonna eat as much Filipino food as we can in their massive food court. [00:09-00:13] First up for $995 they gave us a mountain of pan-seed beehun noodles. [00:13-00:16] I think the noodles literally weighed 5 pounds. [00:16-00:17] I love how there's these fish balls on top. [00:17-00:21] And there was so much noodles that we were already full after eating this. [00:21-00:24] But we saw this food stall stocking up on Filipino street food. [00:24-00:26] So for $6.99 we got the pork shiomai. [00:26-00:28] And we got a veggie lumpia for $3. [00:28-00:30] That lumpia was crispy and fresh. [00:30-00:31] Pretty good deal for $3. [00:31-00:34] So we saw you can get chicken and dobo here in pork blood stew. [00:34-00:35] So you know we had to get some. [00:35-00:38] They come with a box of rice and a chicken or barbecue pork skewer. [00:38-00:41] These combos cost us $14.99 and $16.50. [00:41-00:44] And that pork skewer was probably the best thing I've had so far. [00:44-00:46] But this is my first time eating pork blood stew. [00:46-00:48] The pork was tender and I love the vinegar taste. [00:48-00:50] And I love how there's these quail eggs in the chicken and dobo. [00:50-00:53] And the chicken and dobo had a nice balance of sweet savory and sour. [00:53-00:55] But we can't leave without eating dessert. [00:55-00:59] So for $8.95 we got my favorite Filipino dessert, hallow hallow.

items.address: 3300 Danforth Ave, Scarborough, ON M1L 1C4, Canada
items.caption: My friend @Chopstick Travel first time in Toronto, and he asked me to bring him on my Toronto Food Tour ⬇️ 1) @Momo Ghar Toronto with owner chef Sonam, he comes from a fine-dining culinary background, you can see the finesse and technique he puts into his dishes yet his dishes are also welcoming and comforting  2) @Vietnoms.ca🇨🇦🇻🇳 - @Tony H opened this spot with his mom to celebrate and share his mom’s cooking. Love this spot! Thank you to the follower who spent 30 minutes in the ttc convincing me to try this place!  3) @pacificmalltoronto Dumpling House - don’t think this dumpling master has an IG but you can find him every day, making the same consistently delicious dumplings by hand in p mall 4) @Chloe’s Convenience - one of the first food videos I ever made over 2 years ago, love their sesame flavoured sauces and high quality toppings. So good! Please do go and support them, they work incredibly hard and it’s affordable, delicious and refreshing noodles  5) I'm a little hot crab invited Luke over. It was my first time having Singaporean Chilli crab and it didn’t disappoint! Live @BC Dungeness Crab and live PEI lobsters, it’s as fresh as it gets. Loved all the flavours but I think the salted duck egg yolk cream sauce crab was my favourite! #torontofood #toronto #tibetanfood #chinesefood #vietnamesefood #noodles #dumplings #foodies #eating
items.transcript: [00:00-00:03] If you only had one day in Toronto, here's where I would take you to eat. [00:03-00:05] It's my friend Luke's first time in Toronto. [00:05-00:08] So we ate everything from cheap eats to the busiest bites. [00:08-00:10] Taking off this food tour, I brought Luke to my favorite Momo spot. [00:10-00:12] I love the Momo here so much. [00:12-00:15] That Momo Guard was in my top 5 bites of 2024. [00:15-00:18] I'm obsessed with their cabbage Momo and Tendori Momo. [00:18-00:23] That tangy spice of the Tendori Momo, it's so satisfying that it got us hugging each other. [00:23-00:25] But if Momo Guard is in my top 2024, [00:25-00:28] I wanted to bring Luke to a spot that might make it to my top 2025. [00:28-00:30] Viet Nam's, and here's why. [00:30-00:33] Everything here is made from scratch with high quality ingredients. [00:33-00:35] But this is the dish I always come back for. [00:35-00:37] There's sizzling plate of beef, egg, and meatballs. [00:37-00:39] Everything on this plate is made from scratch. [00:39-00:42] And that homemade patay and tomato sauce is so addictive. [00:42-00:44] But I also wanted to bring Luke to Pimaal. [00:44-00:48] Not just because it has cheap eats, but because it holds a special place in my heart. [00:48-00:51] Pimaal was one of the first food videos I made over two years ago. [00:51-00:54] And this uncle who makes all his dumplings by hand, he's still there. [00:54-00:57] He's still making the same crispy, juicy dumplings. [00:57-00:58] They're so fresh. [00:58-01:01] And so comforting to eat, that we burnt our mouths eating them. [01:01-01:04] And I was so happy to see that Chloe's convenience was still here. [01:04-01:07] They first opened over two years ago, serving Hong Kong-style bag noodles. [01:07-01:10] I love how you can pick the toppings you want in your noodles. [01:10-01:12] And they mix it all together in their signature sauce. [01:12-01:15] The noodles are just so refreshing to eat, perfect for a summer day. [01:15-01:17] And I love the sesame flavor in their sauce. [01:17-01:21] But then Luke turned the tables on me and brought me to a Singaporean chili crab spot he's been [01:21-01:22] wanting to try. [01:22-01:26] And apparently some claim that chili crab is better here than in Singapore. [01:26-01:29] Because they use fresh Canadian lobster and fresh Canadian crab. [01:29-01:32] Definitely not cheap, but I love the chili crab sauce here. [01:32-01:35] And with live lobster and live crab, it's as fresh as you can get.

items.address: 33 Elm St, Toronto, ON M5G 1H1, Canada
items.caption: Living my best life before this app gets banned (splurging 160 EUR on the most recommended seafood restaurant in Cervejaria Ramiro) @visitportugal  #lisboa #portugal #seafood #foodies #eating
items.transcript: [00:00-00:02] This is what 160 euros gets you. [00:02-00:06] At one of the most recommended seafood restaurants in Lisbon. [00:06-00:09] It's so popular, even Mark Wins and Anthony Bourdain have been here. [00:09-00:13] First up, for 68 euros, we got a whole edible crab. [00:13-00:16] The crab head is filled with its creamy head butter, [00:16-00:18] but the thing that shocked me was just how big those claws were. [00:18-00:22] They were the size of my face, and I love how it's already broken for you, [00:22-00:24] so all you gotta do is enjoy that sweet, delicious flesh. [00:24-00:27] But when I saw it just how big their tiger prawns were, [00:27-00:29] I bought one for 23 euros. [00:29-00:33] It comes with a reddish head butter, and I was happy that the muscular flesh was so long, [00:33-00:34] it was longer than my head. [00:34-00:38] The flesh was nice and sweet, and I thought this was actually worth it for 23 euros. [00:38-00:40] But this is the dish everyone comes here for. [00:40-00:42] The iconic grilled scarlet prawns. [00:42-00:45] Serve table-side, the head butter is just insane. [00:45-00:47] They slather your meat with that head butter. [00:47-00:49] It's so rich, it's so creamy. [00:49-00:53] I enjoyed it so much that I ate it with bread, and sucked it straight from the head. [00:53-00:57] For 45 euros, this is definitely not cheap, but when are you gonna be in Lisbon again?

items.address: 214 King St W Suite 100, Toronto, ON M5H 3S6, Canada
items.caption: Seafood Tower 🦞🦐🐙 @Lobster Burger Bar #seafood #lobster
items.transcript: [00:00-00:05] I'm eating a whole gigantic seafood tower, layered with three lobsters, fresh clams and muscles, [00:05-00:09] and a mountain of octopus and shrimp. It's a marathon not a sprint, so I'm starring at the [00:09-00:14] bottom with those muscular lobster tails. So good I had to add another lobster tail drenched in [00:14-00:18] their delicious melted butter. There's probably nothing more decadent in lobster and butter, [00:18-00:23] but I have to keep on making my way up the seafood tower. Next on my journey are those fresh oysters. [00:23-00:27] Naturally sweet and savory, you don't have to put anything on them. And the final boss is this [00:27-00:32] mountain of octopus and shrimp. The shrimp are nice and plump, but the octopus was next level. [00:32-00:41] I know they're intelligent beings, but why do they have to taste so delicious?

items.address: 5 Glen Cameron Rd #27, Thornhill, ON L3T 5W2, Canada
items.caption: Hey folks, highly recommend you try @Piggy’s Island ⬇️ @Vietnoms🇨🇦🇻🇳 and I are huge fans of this spot and would love to see it thriving. Please support if you can! In my opinion, if you had to try one thing here, you must try their new  1) 뼈구이 +주먹밥 Spicy Grilled Pork Backbone + Rice Ball (Gamjatang Style) $39.99 Marinated pork neck bones grilled to perfection with a spicy Korean kick, inspired by traditional Gamjatang flavors. You get a gigantic mountain of Pork!  Other items featured: 2) Korean Cast Iron Pot Lid Pork Belly ($24.99 per person, comes with Kimchi and other sides to grill and eat) 3) Braised Beef Short Ribs Sizzling Platter $39.99 4) Assorted Korean Pancakes (theirs meat inside each of them!) $35 5) Crispy Ground Mung Bean Pancake (they ground the Mung Bean with a stone mill on site!) $25 #supportsmallbusiness #koreanfood #koreanbbq #foodies #eating
items.transcript: [00:00-00:01] Today is a sad day. [00:01-00:03] Because one of my favorite Korean restaurants [00:03-00:04] told me they're struggling to stay open. [00:04-00:06] They haven't been able to pay rent. [00:06-00:07] And fear the landlord may kick them out. [00:07-00:09] So today, my friend Tony and I [00:09-00:11] are gonna share with you why we're big fans of this restaurant. [00:11-00:13] When we got there for lunch, it was completely empty. [00:13-00:16] But we started off with the pork belly barbecue combos. [00:16-00:17] It's $25 per person. [00:17-00:19] It comes with pork belly and a bunch of sides. [00:19-00:21] I absolutely love the homemade kimchi. [00:21-00:23] You grill it and then combine it with the pork belly. [00:23-00:24] It's an incredible bite. [00:24-00:26] The pork here is like really good. [00:26-00:27] One of my favorite, I would get pork here. [00:27-00:28] The quality of the meat here is amazing. [00:28-00:30] The eggs were trying to spicy pork backbone. [00:30-00:32] They slow cooked the pork bone for hours. [00:32-00:35] Then they finish it on the grill with their signature spicy sauce. [00:35-00:36] That pork was so tender. [00:36-00:38] It's just disintegrated in my mouth. [00:38-00:40] And that fatty tender pork, there's just something [00:40-00:43] so satisfying about combining it with the rice ball. [00:43-00:45] I've never had anything like this so good. [00:48-00:50] This is the best thing I've had in this place ever. [00:50-00:52] We also got to try their braised beef short ribs. [00:52-00:54] Also, fall off the bone tender. [00:54-00:56] What I love about this place is that the food [00:56-00:57] is really good quality. [00:57-00:59] They make everything from scratch. [00:59-01:01] Their price point for the quality, I think, is very fair. [01:01-01:02] I would love for you to try this place [01:02-01:03] and let me know what you think. [01:03-01:05] They seem to be short staff though, [01:05-01:06] so please be patient with them.

items.address: 370 College St, Toronto, ON M5T 1S6, Canada
items.caption: 28 hours of eating from Toronto to @Kaikoura NZ  - @Air New Zealand airplane food - @Cookie Time  - Lemon & Paeroa - Cooper’s Catch Kaikōura #kaikoura #newzealand #airplanefood #Foodie #eating
items.transcript: [00:00-00:03] This is everything we ate on our 28 hour journey to New Zealand. [00:03-00:08] After 7 hours we arrive in Vancouver to catch our air New Zealand flight to New Zealand. [00:08-00:11] Immediately we were served with an appetizer of salmon from New Zealand. [00:11-00:14] For airplane food this was a surprisingly fresh piece of salmon. [00:14-00:18] So for dinner I decided to double down with their seared deep sea caught fish. [00:18-00:21] The cod is also caught from New Zealand and I love the creaminess of that cod. [00:21-00:24] After 10 hours into our flight it's breakfast time. [00:24-00:28] I got the spinach and cottage cheese omelette and again for airplane food I was super impressed. [00:28-00:32] How did they get the omelette to be so velvety even after reheating? [00:32-00:36] After 21 hours we arrived and learned that the flat white was invented by New Zealand. [00:36-00:37] But our journey isn't over yet. [00:37-00:40] We still had to take a flight from Auckland to Christchurch. [00:40-00:44] And then at Christchurch we were picked up to go to our final destination. [00:44-00:47] Our driver, Brent, welcomed us with a goodie bag full of New Zealand treats. [00:47-00:50] And the view on our drive was absolutely stunning. [00:50-00:52] You have the mountains on one side and the ocean on the other. [00:52-00:56] We got hungry so we stopped by this fish and chip-chack right next to the ocean. [00:56-00:58] They only served fish that is caught on that day. [00:58-01:00] I ordered their deep sea caught and tarot key. [01:00-01:04] They were literally caught hours ago from the ocean right next to the shack. [01:04-01:07] These were some of the freshest fish I've ever had in my life. [01:07-01:10] What a way to start my adventures in Kaikoura, New Zealand. [01:10-01:12] Stay tuned for more videos.

items.address: 5 Glen Cameron Rd #27, Thornhill, ON L3T 5W2, Canada
items.caption: Hey folks, highly recommend you try @Piggy’s Island ⬇️ @Vietnoms🇨🇦🇻🇳 and I are huge fans of this spot and would love to see it thriving. Please support if you can! In my opinion, if you had to try one thing here, you must try their new  1) 뼈구이 +주먹밥 Spicy Grilled Pork Backbone + Rice Ball (Gamjatang Style) $39.99 Marinated pork neck bones grilled to perfection with a spicy Korean kick, inspired by traditional Gamjatang flavors. You get a gigantic mountain of Pork!  Other items featured: 2) Korean Cast Iron Pot Lid Pork Belly ($24.99 per person, comes with Kimchi and other sides to grill and eat) 3) Braised Beef Short Ribs Sizzling Platter $39.99 4) Assorted Korean Pancakes (theirs meat inside each of them!) $35 5) Crispy Ground Mung Bean Pancake (they ground the Mung Bean with a stone mill on site!) $25 #supportsmallbusiness #koreanfood #koreanbbq #foodies #eating
items.transcript: [00:00-00:01] Today is a sad day. [00:01-00:03] Because one of my favorite Korean restaurants [00:03-00:04] told me they're struggling to stay open. [00:04-00:06] They haven't been able to pay rent. [00:06-00:07] And fear the landlord may kick them out. [00:07-00:09] So today, my friend Tony and I [00:09-00:11] are gonna share with you why we're big fans of this restaurant. [00:11-00:13] When we got there for lunch, it was completely empty. [00:13-00:16] But we started off with the pork belly barbecue combos. [00:16-00:17] It's $25 per person. [00:17-00:19] It comes with pork belly and a bunch of sides. [00:19-00:21] I absolutely love the homemade kimchi. [00:21-00:23] You grill it and then combine it with the pork belly. [00:23-00:24] It's an incredible bite. [00:24-00:26] The pork here is like really good. [00:26-00:27] One of my favorite, I would get pork here. [00:27-00:28] The quality of the meat here is amazing. [00:28-00:30] The eggs were trying to spicy pork backbone. [00:30-00:32] They slow cooked the pork bone for hours. [00:32-00:35] Then they finish it on the grill with their signature spicy sauce. [00:35-00:36] That pork was so tender. [00:36-00:38] It's just disintegrated in my mouth. [00:38-00:40] And that fatty tender pork, there's just something [00:40-00:43] so satisfying about combining it with the rice ball. [00:43-00:45] I've never had anything like this so good. [00:48-00:50] This is the best thing I've had in this place ever. [00:50-00:52] We also got to try their braised beef short ribs. [00:52-00:54] Also, fall off the bone tender. [00:54-00:56] What I love about this place is that the food [00:56-00:57] is really good quality. [00:57-00:59] They make everything from scratch. [00:59-01:01] Their price point for the quality, I think, is very fair. [01:01-01:02] I would love for you to try this place [01:02-01:03] and let me know what you think. [01:03-01:05] They seem to be short staff though, [01:05-01:06] so please be patient with them.

items.address: 25 Curity Ave, East York, ON M4B 3M2, Canada
items.caption: Toronto is home to one of the largest Portuguese communities outside of Portugal ⬇️ My Portuguese friend @Crosstown Craft BBQ shows me some of his favourite Portuguese food spots in the city: 1) @Bonsi Sandwiches $12 gigantic Bifanas served out of a food truck in a parking lot 2) @Casa Portuguesa piri piri chicken and Francesinha 3) Cafe Belem Toronto for Pastel de Nata #portuguesefood #toronto #Foodie #eating #pasteldenata
items.transcript: [00:00-00:04] Where do Portuguese people actually go to eat when they're craving Portuguese food in Toronto? [00:04-00:06] I've had some of the best Portuguese food in Portugal. [00:06-00:09] So today I'm with my Portuguese friend Rubin to find out how good [00:09-00:13] Toronto's Portuguese food is. First up we're at a food truck in the corner of this parking lot, [00:13-00:18] run by a single Portuguese man who makes a beefana, inspired by a recipe he discovered in his family's [00:18-00:23] hometown. And his beefanash is stuffed with pork meat. He makes them two ways, the bonzi way, [00:23-00:27] where he stuffs it with additional potato chips, and he also makes a more traditional beefana. [00:27-00:31] This is by far the biggest beefana I've ever had in my life. The bun is fresh, [00:31-00:35] and the meat is super tender and fused with flavor. This is the second best beefana I've ever had. [00:35-00:40] Right behind a fanzo in Lisbon. Next up we're at this Portuguese restaurant where many of the dishes [00:40-00:44] are prepared by Portuguese grandmas. And we're here to answer one question. How does the [00:44-00:48] PDPD chicken here compare to the ones in Portugal? After slicing up their chicken, we drench [00:48-00:53] the chicken in their homemade PDPD sauce. That PDPD sauce is so garlicky. It adds an incredible [00:53-00:57] aroma to that chicken. And because of that sauce, I think it's better than Bonjardin in Lisbon. [00:57-01:01] But I almost got cancelled in Portugal for eating a francincenia. So today I'm eating the [01:01-01:06] Francincenia again. They make it here very similar to the ones I've had in Porto. I love the Yolquie [01:06-01:10] Agontop. It was so cheesy, so decadent. And I've learned my lesson. Never again, well I eat a [01:10-01:15] Francincenia with my hands. But I have yet to find a Pashtez de Nata in Toronto as good as the ones [01:15-01:19] I've had in Portugal. But Ruben claims despot makes really good Pashtez de Nata. The owner totes [01:19-01:24] they spent months perfecting this recipe. And they make them every 30 minutes in small batches [01:24-01:28] to make sure it's always fresh. The pastry is flaky and fluffy. The filling is very [01:28-01:35] custardy. A very strong Pashtez de Nata. Right up there with the best I've had in Portugal.

items.address: 257 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M5V 1Z4, Canada
items.caption: Thai Hong Kong Style Cafe Restaurant Levels Up My Ramen (📍Yeah Yeah Thai)
items.transcript: [00:00-00:02] I'm challenging restaurants to level up my ramen. [00:02-00:08] And today my wife and I are gonna see how this Hong Kong style Thai cuisine restaurant is going to make my ramen gourmet. [00:08-00:11] But first we're gonna have a Hong Kong style Thai cuisine feast. [00:11-00:15] I tried to eat their chicken wing in one bite by burnt myself, but I've never tried it as quite like this one. [00:15-00:17] Cheesy, creamy, tamion, risotto. [00:17-00:19] The flavor is like nothing I've ever had before. [00:19-00:22] You get the sweet and sour from the tamion and creaminess from the cheese. [00:22-00:23] Absolutely stunning. [00:23-00:27] And I have fond memories of this cheesy, big chicken spaghetti because I grew up eating this. [00:27-00:32] But what's most surprising about this version is there's green curry in it, which I actually love. [00:32-00:35] And this might be my wife's favorite dish, mango sticky rice. [00:35-00:37] Eating it brought us back to our time in Thailand. [00:37-00:44] But the moment you've been waiting for this restaurant leveled up my ramen by adding their signature creamy tamion sauce to instant ramen. [00:44-00:46] That creamy tamion sauce absolutely blew my mind.

items.address: 370 College St, Toronto, ON M5T 1S6, Canada
items.caption: 28 hours of eating from Toronto to @Kaikoura NZ  - @Air New Zealand airplane food - @Cookie Time  - Lemon & Paeroa - Cooper’s Catch Kaikōura #kaikoura #newzealand #airplanefood #Foodie #eating
items.transcript: [00:00-00:03] This is everything we ate on our 28 hour journey to New Zealand. [00:03-00:08] After 7 hours we arrive in Vancouver to catch our air New Zealand flight to New Zealand. [00:08-00:11] Immediately we were served with an appetizer of salmon from New Zealand. [00:11-00:14] For airplane food this was a surprisingly fresh piece of salmon. [00:14-00:18] So for dinner I decided to double down with their seared deep sea caught fish. [00:18-00:21] The cod is also caught from New Zealand and I love the creaminess of that cod. [00:21-00:24] After 10 hours into our flight it's breakfast time. [00:24-00:28] I got the spinach and cottage cheese omelette and again for airplane food I was super impressed. [00:28-00:32] How did they get the omelette to be so velvety even after reheating? [00:32-00:36] After 21 hours we arrived and learned that the flat white was invented by New Zealand. [00:36-00:37] But our journey isn't over yet. [00:37-00:40] We still had to take a flight from Auckland to Christchurch. [00:40-00:44] And then at Christchurch we were picked up to go to our final destination. [00:44-00:47] Our driver, Brent, welcomed us with a goodie bag full of New Zealand treats. [00:47-00:50] And the view on our drive was absolutely stunning. [00:50-00:52] You have the mountains on one side and the ocean on the other. [00:52-00:56] We got hungry so we stopped by this fish and chip-chack right next to the ocean. [00:56-00:58] They only served fish that is caught on that day. [00:58-01:00] I ordered their deep sea caught and tarot key. [01:00-01:04] They were literally caught hours ago from the ocean right next to the shack. [01:04-01:07] These were some of the freshest fish I've ever had in my life. [01:07-01:10] What a way to start my adventures in Kaikoura, New Zealand. [01:10-01:12] Stay tuned for more videos.

items.address: 1446 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M6K 1M2
items.caption: @Ali Tahinis shows me his favorite noodle spot with @Jesse Jaurji @mikakuudonbar #noodles #hiddengems #udon
items.transcript: [00:00-00:03] asking famous creators to bring me to their favorite restaurants in the city. [00:03-00:06] And today, Ali Tahini's is bringing me to his favorite noodle spot. [00:06-00:10] One thing that you have to order here, you have to try the chicken curry udon. [00:10-00:12] That's what I wanted to order. [00:12-00:16] As per Ali Tahini's instructions, we came here for one dish and one dish only. [00:16-00:17] The chicken curry udon. items.address: 794 Dundas St W, Toronto, ON M6J 1V1
items.caption: If you are visiting Toronto and only have time for one restaurant, @Patois Toronto by Chef Craig Wong is where I would bring you: we brought our friend Grace Chan to try Jamaican Chinese food for the first time  @ghlchan @Munchie Peachy  #jamaicanfood #chinesefood #asianfood #toronto #Foodie
items.transcript: [00:00-00:03] If you could only have one meal in Toronto, here's where I would bring you to eat. [00:03-00:05] My friend Grace is visiting from out of town. [00:05-00:06] She asked me for something different. [00:06-00:09] So I decided to bring her to the patchwell for Jamaican Chinese food. [00:09-00:11] I think Toronto has some of the best Jamaican Chinese food. [00:11-00:12] And not easy to find anywhere else. [00:12-00:15] You start off with one of the most recommended dishes from the staff. [00:15-00:16] The jerk pork belly, yakisoba. [00:16-00:19] The noodles are walk-tossed into signature oxtail gravy. [00:19-00:22] And the pork belly is slathered with her homemade jerk sauce. [00:22-00:24] It was spicy, it was vibrant. [00:24-00:25] And I think Grace liked it too. [00:25-00:26] Oh my God! [00:26-00:27] Holy crap! [00:27-00:29] And for me, this is one of my favorite dishes of the night. [00:29-00:30] Popcorn shrimp fried rice. [00:30-00:33] The fried rice is infused with loads of shrimp flavor. [00:33-00:36] It had that natural sweetness and umami from the shrimp. [00:36-00:38] Grace told me she isn't the biggest fan of shrimp, but even she liked it. [00:38-00:40] But this is probably the craziest dish of them all. [00:40-00:42] They're Chinese pineapple bun burgers. [00:42-00:43] You get the classic pineapple bun. [00:43-00:46] But with some of the juiciest burger patties I've ever seen in my life. [00:46-00:50] It was so juicy that I had to lick the meat juices off my fingers. [00:50-00:52] Oh my God! [00:52-00:53] Is it good? [00:53-00:55] And that's why I love bringing people to patchwell. [00:55-00:59] For me, the flavors are familiar, but I love how they blend flavors and cultures together. [00:59-01:00] You create something completely different and new.

items.address: 4810 Sheppard Ave E, Scarborough, ON M1S 4N6

items.address: 100 King St W, Toronto, ON M5X 1C9, Canada
items.caption: $20 Food Challenge Scarborough Edition 📍Al Premium 📍Samosa King 📍Drupati’s Roti & Doubles 📍Bread King Bakeries
items.transcript: [00:00-00:04] This is how much food $20 gets you in one of the most diverse areas in Toronto. [00:04-00:10] We drove to the four corners of Scarborough to try four of the most iconic and affordable Scarborough food spots. [00:10-00:15] First up, for only $6.99, I've never seen so much noodles piled into one single bowl. [00:15-00:19] We got this at an Asian grocery store called Al Premium and I think it was like three pounds of noodles. [00:19-00:24] But we had to drive almost 30 minutes to our next spot because we wanted some samosas at samosa king. [00:24-00:29] This place is so popular that there's always a line up and they're constantly making samosas so you know they're fresh. [00:29-00:33] But I couldn't believe the price for only $2.00, you get six samosas that's unbeatable. [00:33-00:37] But my friend, the Scarborough man, told me that this was his go-to place while he was growing up. [00:37-00:39] It's a place country potty. [00:39-00:42] And for $5.95 we got a doubles loaded with goat meat. [00:42-00:44] There was so much goat meat that it was falling out of the doubles. [00:44-00:47] And I absolutely love how fresh the flatbread was. [00:47-00:49] And last but not least, we had to get some dessert. [00:49-00:53] At only 50 cents a pastry, these are some of the most affordable pastries I've ever seen. [00:53-00:57] But we ended up smirging a bit by spending $3.49 on a cream horn pastry.

You get a gigantic mountain of Pork!  Other items featured: 2) Korean Cast Iron Pot Lid Pork Belly ($24.99 per person, comes with Kimchi and other sides to grill and eat) 3) Braised Beef Short Ribs Sizzling Platter $39.99 4) Assorted Korean Pancakes (theirs meat inside each of them!) $35 5) Crispy Ground Mung Bean Pancake (they ground the Mung Bean with a stone mill on site!) $25 #supportsmallbusiness #koreanfood #koreanbbq #foodies #eating
items.transcript: [00:00-00:01] Today is a sad day. [00:01-00:03] Because one of my favorite Korean restaurants [00:03-00:04] told me they're struggling to stay open. [00:04-00:06] They haven't been able to pay rent. [00:06-00:07] And fear the landlord may kick them out. [00:07-00:09] So today, my friend Tony and I [00:09-00:11] are gonna share with you why we're big fans of this restaurant. [00:11-00:13] When we got there for lunch, it was completely empty. [00:13-00:16] But we started off with the pork belly barbecue combos. [00:16-00:17] It's $25 per person. [00:17-00:19] It comes with pork belly and a bunch of sides. [00:19-00:21] I absolutely love the homemade kimchi. [00:21-00:23] You grill it and then combine it with the pork belly. [00:23-00:24] It's an incredible bite. [00:24-00:26] The pork here is like really good. [00:26-00:27] One of my favorite, I would get pork here. [00:27-00:28] The quality of the meat here is amazing. [00:28-00:30] The eggs were trying to spicy pork backbone. [00:30-00:32] They slow cooked the pork bone for hours. [00:32-00:35] Then they finish it on the grill with their signature spicy sauce. [00:35-00:36] That pork was so tender. [00:36-00:38] It's just disintegrated in my mouth. [00:38-00:40] And that fatty tender pork, there's just something [00:40-00:43] so satisfying about combining it with the rice ball. [00:43-00:45] I've never had anything like this so good. [00:48-00:50] This is the best thing I've had in this place ever. [00:50-00:52] We also got to try their braised beef short ribs. [00:52-00:54] Also, fall off the bone tender. [00:54-00:56] What I love about this place is that the food [00:56-00:57] is really good quality. [00:57-00:59] They make everything from scratch. [00:59-01:01] Their price point for the quality, I think, is very fair. [01:01-01:02] I would love for you to try this place [01:02-01:03] and let me know what you think. [01:03-01:05] They seem to be short staff though, [01:05-01:06] so please be patient with them.

items.caption: Toronto is home to one of the largest Portuguese communities outside of Portugal ⬇️ My Portuguese friend @Crosstown Craft BBQ shows me some of his favourite Portuguese food spots in the city: 1) @Bonsi Sandwiches $12 gigantic Bifanas served out of a food truck in a parking lot 2) @Casa Portuguesa piri piri chicken and Francesinha 3) Cafe Belem Toronto for Pastel de Nata #portuguesefood #toronto #Foodie #eating #pasteldenata
items.transcript: [00:00-00:04] Where do Portuguese people actually go to eat when they're craving Portuguese food in Toronto? [00:04-00:06] I've had some of the best Portuguese food in Portugal. [00:06-00:09] So today I'm with my Portuguese friend Rubin to find out how good [00:09-00:13] Toronto's Portuguese food is. First up we're at a food truck in the corner of this parking lot, [00:13-00:18] run by a single Portuguese man who makes a beefana, inspired by a recipe he discovered in his family's [00:18-00:23] hometown. And his beefanash is stuffed with pork meat. He makes them two ways, the bonzi way, [00:23-00:27] where he stuffs it with additional potato chips, and he also makes a more traditional beefana. [00:27-00:31] This is by far the biggest beefana I've ever had in my life. The bun is fresh, [00:31-00:35] and the meat is super tender and fused with flavor. This is the second best beefana I've ever had. [00:35-00:40] Right behind a fanzo in Lisbon. Next up we're at this Portuguese restaurant where many of the dishes [00:40-00:44] are prepared by Portuguese grandmas. And we're here to answer one question. How does the [00:44-00:48] PDPD chicken here compare to the ones in Portugal? After slicing up their chicken, we drench [00:48-00:53] the chicken in their homemade PDPD sauce. That PDPD sauce is so garlicky. It adds an incredible [00:53-00:57] aroma to that chicken. And because of that sauce, I think it's better than Bonjardin in Lisbon. [00:57-01:01] But I almost got cancelled in Portugal for eating a francincenia. So today I'm eating the [01:01-01:06] Francincenia again. They make it here very similar to the ones I've had in Porto. I love the Yolquie [01:06-01:10] Agontop. It was so cheesy, so decadent. And I've learned my lesson. Never again, well I eat a [01:10-01:15] Francincenia with my hands. But I have yet to find a Pashtez de Nata in Toronto as good as the ones [01:15-01:19] I've had in Portugal. But Ruben claims despot makes really good Pashtez de Nata. The owner totes [01:19-01:24] they spent months perfecting this recipe. And they make them every 30 minutes in small batches [01:24-01:28] to make sure it's always fresh. The pastry is flaky and fluffy. The filling is very [01:28-01:35] custardy. A very strong Pashtez de Nata. Right up there with the best I've had in Portugal.

items.transcript: [00:00-00:04] This is how much food you can get for $20 here in Toronto at this hidden gem food court. [00:04-00:08] First up, extremely juicy, pan-fried pork bun dumplings. [00:08-00:14] They cost $8.19 and I am blown away by how consistently juicy these dumplings were. [00:14-00:17] These are my new favorite dumplings and you definitely need to clean up after yourself. [00:17-00:20] Next up, Chinese crepes for $8.95. [00:20-00:24] My wife is super excited to try these crepes because these are crepes she ate while growing up. [00:24-00:28] There's some crunchy things and some sausages in this one, but each vendor has their own style. [00:28-00:32] Eating this crepe, my wife is reminded her for childhood and we love how they make fresh to order. [00:32-00:35] Lastly, for $4.50 we got this cocoa bread patty from IWINS. items.transcript: [00:00-00:03] So you remember how I got a mountain of chicken and fried rice for 750? [00:03-00:07] Today I'm going back to the same restaurant to see how much food a hundred dollars would get. [00:07-00:12] I'm trying to create a meal for my family for a hundred dollars, but whatever the restaurant owner likes. [00:12-00:13] Oh, okay. [00:13-00:14] Whoa, it's seven items. [00:14-00:15] Yeah. [00:15-00:19] So the bill came out to 98, 96 before tax, and there was a lot of food. [00:19-00:23] The food was so heavy that I had to order a Uber just to get the food home. [00:23-00:23] Oh my god, what's really happening? [00:23-00:24] What is this? [00:24-00:26] I don't know, I just told them to get me a lot of food. [00:26-00:28] You don't even know what it is. [00:28-00:31] The manager ended up ordering me chicken wings, pepper shrimp, fried rice, [00:31-00:34] hawka noodles with I think shrimp and chicken, chili beef with gravy, [00:34-00:37] kung-pang chicken, and mixed vegetables. [00:37-00:41] There was so much food that even with five people, we were able to load our plates full of food, [00:41-00:43] and we barely scratched a dent. items.address: 93 Ossington Ave, Toronto, ON M6J 2Z2, Canada
items.caption: Saying YES to every restaurant that invites me @Munchie Peachy  1) Lunch Lady Toronto  2) @Oklahoma Burgers  3) @Queens Harbour  #toronto #foodies #eating #japanesefood #vietnamesefood #burgers
items.transcript: [00:00-00:03] Today I'm saying yes to every restaurant that invites me. [00:03-00:07] And the last one is one of the most extravagant eating experiences of 2025. [00:07-00:11] So I started off the day by going to Anthony Bourdain's favorite Vietnamese restaurant, Lunch Lady. [00:11-00:15] They let us try the whole entire dinner menu, but one of my favorite dishes is their steak looklack. [00:15-00:20] It's Ontario Prime Ribbye, it melts in your mouth, and I love that buttery peppercorn sauce. [00:20-00:22] I enjoy it, they're fuccicious so much I ask for seconds. [00:22-00:26] It's a scallion butter with fish sauce, combined with a phoagwap hotay. [00:26-00:28] I ate like five of these, but this is my favorite dessert of the night. [00:28-00:31] It's a pandan sticky rice cake with coconut condensed milk. [00:31-00:33] Soft and chewing, I love that pandan flavor. [00:33-00:35] But up next I'm trying Oklahoma burgers. [00:35-00:39] Apparently there is half a pound of meat in this burger, and they infuse it with a bone marrow sauce. [00:39-00:42] Solid burger, but the key move for me is that bone marrow sauce. [00:42-00:46] Finally, this is the most extravagant restaurant I've been to in 2025. [00:46-00:48] You walk in and there is a sushi station. [00:48-00:52] With a whole blue fin tuna carved up and a chef onsite on demand feeding you. [00:52-00:54] Then I bumped into this guy's shocking oysters. [00:54-00:56] I was tempted to get 48 oysters. [00:56-00:58] But I didn't realize I was just at the start of the restaurant. [00:58-01:02] In the middle of the restaurant are 50 chefs constantly churning out odors. [01:02-01:06] There was everything from sliders to fried chicken, meat skewers, [01:06-01:08] but my favorite were these Aaron Cheney balls. [01:08-01:10] But the crazy part is there is even more to this restaurant. [01:10-01:12] There's an entire restaurant in the back. [01:12-01:14] Where two chefs are constantly carving prime ribs. [01:14-01:16] They have different sauces you can dip your prime rib in. [01:16-01:18] The chimichurri sauce was the key move for me. [01:18-01:20] Overall, a very long day of eating.

items.address: 214 King St W Suite 100, Toronto, ON M5H 3S6, Canada
items.caption: Seafood Tower 🦞🦐🐙 @Lobster Burger Bar #seafood #lobster
items.transcript: [00:00-00:05] I'm eating a whole gigantic seafood tower, layered with three lobsters, fresh clams and muscles, [00:05-00:09] and a mountain of octopus and shrimp. It's a marathon not a sprint, so I'm starring at the [00:09-00:14] bottom with those muscular lobster tails. So good I had to add another lobster tail drenched in [00:14-00:18] their delicious melted butter. There's probably nothing more decadent in lobster and butter, [00:18-00:23] but I have to keep on making my way up the seafood tower. Next on my journey are those fresh oysters. [00:23-00:27] Naturally sweet and savory, you don't have to put anything on them. And the final boss is this [00:27-00:32] mountain of octopus and shrimp. The shrimp are nice and plump, but the octopus was next level. [00:32-00:41] I know they're intelligent beings, but why do they have to taste so delicious?

items.address: 81 Bay St. 4th Floor, Toronto, ON M5J 0E7, Canada
items.caption: Went from eating $500 worth of Japanese Wagyu to eating $9 Aloette Go Burger @Aloette Go
items.transcript: [00:00-00:05] What a surprise of a lifetime. Michelin star chef, Patrick Cris prepared a wagyu feast for me. [00:05-00:09] There was wagyu tartar with caviar, wagyu tataki and wagyu ribeye. [00:09-00:12] As my hero Mark Wings would say, it's so tender you don't need teeth to eat this. [00:12-00:18] That wagyu feast filled me with so much joy that I decided to go to Aloha go to try Patrick Cris' burger. [00:18-00:20] And what makes their burger so special is that cheese. [00:20-00:25] It's a slice of cheddar cheese that has been caramelized on a grill and infused in that beef patty. [00:25-00:28] That caramelized cheese is a game changer. [00:28-00:30] And when I get go, you know the food may be happy. [00:30-00:33] But this might be the biggest crispy fried chicken I've ever seen. [00:33-00:36] When I try to eat this chicken burger, it barely fit in my mouth. [00:36-00:37] Absolutely stunning.

items.caption: Hey folks, highly recommend you try @Piggy’s Island ⬇️ @Vietnoms🇨🇦🇻🇳 and I are huge fans of this spot and would love to see it thriving. Please support if you can! In my opinion, if you had to try one thing here, you must try their new  1) 뼈구이 +주먹밥 Spicy Grilled Pork Backbone + Rice Ball (Gamjatang Style) $39.99 Marinated pork neck bones grilled to perfection with a spicy Korean kick, inspired by traditional Gamjatang flavors. You get a gigantic mountain of Pork!  Other items featured: 2) Korean Cast Iron Pot Lid Pork Belly ($24.99 per person, comes with Kimchi and other sides to grill and eat) 3) Braised Beef Short Ribs Sizzling Platter $39.99 4) Assorted Korean Pancakes (theirs meat inside each of them!) $35 5) Crispy Ground Mung Bean Pancake (they ground the Mung Bean with a stone mill on site!) $25 #supportsmallbusiness #koreanfood #koreanbbq #foodies #eating
items.transcript: [00:00-00:01] Today is a sad day. [00:01-00:03] Because one of my favorite Korean restaurants [00:03-00:04] told me they're struggling to stay open. [00:04-00:06] They haven't been able to pay rent. [00:06-00:07] And fear the landlord may kick them out. [00:07-00:09] So today, my friend Tony and I [00:09-00:11] are gonna share with you why we're big fans of this restaurant. [00:11-00:13] When we got there for lunch, it was completely empty. [00:13-00:16] But we started off with the pork belly barbecue combos. [00:16-00:17] It's $25 per person. [00:17-00:19] It comes with pork belly and a bunch of sides. [00:19-00:21] I absolutely love the homemade kimchi. [00:21-00:23] You grill it and then combine it with the pork belly. [00:23-00:24] It's an incredible bite. [00:24-00:26] The pork here is like really good. [00:26-00:27] One of my favorite, I would get pork here. [00:27-00:28] The quality of the meat here is amazing. [00:28-00:30] The eggs were trying to spicy pork backbone. [00:30-00:32] They slow cooked the pork bone for hours. [00:32-00:35] Then they finish it on the grill with their signature spicy sauce. [00:35-00:36] That pork was so tender. [00:36-00:38] It's just disintegrated in my mouth. [00:38-00:40] And that fatty tender pork, there's just something [00:40-00:43] so satisfying about combining it with the rice ball. [00:43-00:45] I've never had anything like this so good. [00:48-00:50] This is the best thing I've had in this place ever. [00:50-00:52] We also got to try their braised beef short ribs. [00:52-00:54] Also, fall off the bone tender. [00:54-00:56] What I love about this place is that the food [00:56-00:57] is really good quality. [00:57-00:59] They make everything from scratch. [00:59-01:01] Their price point for the quality, I think, is very fair. [01:01-01:02] I would love for you to try this place [01:02-01:03] and let me know what you think. [01:03-01:05] They seem to be short staff though, [01:05-01:06] so please be patient with them.

items.caption: Would you airfry your steak?  @Ninja Kitchen  @mccaincanada  @Woodward Meat Purveyors  @Munchie Peachy  #airfryerrecipes #steaktiktok #EasyRecipes #foodhacks
items.transcript: [00:00-00:01] How do you prepare your steaks? [00:01-00:05] I use an air fryer and cook it from frozen covered in salt and an air fryer. [00:05-00:06] Frozen? That's the air fryer hack. [00:06-00:09] The best way to cook your steaks is in the air fryer. [00:09-00:13] Let's try it out. As per the instructions, I got a New York steak frozen. [00:13-00:18] And I just got my new Ninja air fryer. It's got two baskets and I love how you can set [00:18-00:20] two different temperatures and two different air fryer times. [00:20-00:23] So I'm also making some fries to go with my New York steak. [00:23-00:25] As per the video after nine minutes, I checked on the steak. [00:25-00:28] So far, it's actually not looking too bad. [00:28-00:31] And she says to flip the steak and cook it for nine or nine minutes. [00:31-00:32] My fries are already done at this point. [00:32-00:35] But after nine minutes, here are the results. [00:35-00:36] That steak is sizzling. [00:36-00:39] But the big question is how is the middle and how does it taste? [00:39-00:43] The middle is still pink, but to be honest, I like it a little more medium rare. [00:43-00:45] But the taste is absolutely phenomenal. [00:45-00:48] You get a very tender and beefy New York steak. [00:48-00:52] I think next time I might try to cook it six minutes on each side and at a higher temperature.

items.caption: Epic sandwiches at a dinner party hosted by @Mukbang Maxwell and @BEAR Steak Sandwiches - check out Bear Steak Sandwiches for more details on the Maxwelli LeBoeuf Sandwich
items.transcript: [00:00-00:04] Here's everything I ate at a dinner party hosted by Mukbang Maxwell and the founders of [00:04-00:06] BearStake sandwiches. [00:06-00:09] They sous vide a whole Sirloy and Rose for this event because they're about to make a [00:09-00:11] ton of epic sandwiches. [00:11-00:14] What I loved about this dinner party is just how intimate the setting was. [00:14-00:18] Maxwell, Akie and Ricardo were just preparing sandwiches table-side for a saw and it was [00:18-00:21] kind of like a family meal, enjoyed amongst friends. [00:21-00:25] So what makes this dinner party so special is that it's hosted at the house where Bear [00:25-00:26] Stake sandwiches first started. [00:26-00:30] But the coolest part is I get to try to lay the sandwich called the Maxwellie Le Boque. [00:30-00:34] Packed with medium rare slices of beef sirloy and a special in-house made duck sell, this [00:34-00:37] is an incredibly beefy and flavor-packed sandwich. [00:37-00:39] But my favorite part is the juice. [00:39-00:40] The secret ingredient is beef collo. [00:40-00:42] I love this so much I drank it straight.

items.caption: @Air Canada China Flight Food #china #airplanefood
items.transcript: [00:00-00:04] This is everything I ate on my 12-hour flight to China on air Canada. [00:04-00:10] Two hours into our flight we started off with lunch, and I was surprised to find that there's actually a Chinese option and a Western option. [00:10-00:14] I got the Shepers Pie. It was okay. It looks exactly how it tastes a mixture of beef and potatoes. [00:14-00:16] And my wife got the Chinese chicken on rice. [00:16-00:21] Surprisingly, it tasted like tariaki chicken. They also gave us a Chinese cucumber salad. [00:21-00:25] Probably the best thing was the dessert. This brownie was pillowy, soft, moist, and not too sweet. [00:25-00:30] This was definitely the highlight of lunch. But to carry me over to dinner, I watched Mark Wings HBO show. [00:30-00:32] Shout out to Mark Wings for making it to HBO. [00:32-00:35] At six hours into our flight they gave us this tuna sandwich. [00:35-00:37] It was definitely a mid tuna sandwich, but it got the job done. [00:37-00:40] But I was happy to find that it actually came with this Biscoph cookie. [00:40-00:43] These cookies are super addictive. I wish I had more than one. [00:43-00:45] But with only two hours left in our flight, we got breakfast. [00:45-00:47] And I can't believe it, but I got noodles for breakfast. [00:47-00:49] And they were actually good. [00:49-00:50] Love the rice from a chile noodles. [00:50-00:51] And it also had porcanine. [00:51-00:53] My wife decided to get the Western omelette. [00:53-00:55] I liked it, but she said it was just okay. [00:55-00:57] And finally we're in China, so stay tuned for more videos.

items.address: 795 St Clair Ave W, Toronto, ON M6C 1B9, Canada
items.caption: Only eating at Palestinian Restaurants for a full day with @Munchie Peachy 1) @FlamingStove Palestinian Style Shawarma 2) Louf Palestinian Food (invited) 3) @Kunafas  #palestinianfood #palestinian #kunafa #shawarma #eating
items.transcript: [00:00-00:04] Today I'm only eating a Palestinian restaurant, starting with Palestinian-style shawarma, [00:04-00:06] hidden away in the back of this hasty market. [00:06-00:09] But I'm curious, what is Palestinian-style shawarma? [00:09-00:11] The owner told me it all starts with the bread. [00:11-00:16] It's called saj bread, and it's a very thin bread that they make fresh daily, completely from scratch. [00:16-00:19] They put the bread on this dough, and it starts bubbling and cooking within seconds. [00:19-00:21] But the chicken shawarma meat is also very special. [00:21-00:25] It's marinated in Palestinian spices and yogurt for three days, [00:25-00:27] and the key move for me is this ambasauce. [00:27-00:31] It's a tangy, mango-y, vibrant sauce that goes into Palestinian-style shawarma. [00:31-00:36] But these shawarma are so big that even after cutting in a half, it looks like you get two full shawarma. [00:36-00:38] But I've never had a shawarma that tastes like this. [00:38-00:40] The chicken meat is so well seasoned from the spices. [00:40-00:43] The ambasauce gives it an incredible, vibrant kick. [00:43-00:45] This might be my favorite shawarma of all time. [00:45-00:47] For dinner, we went to this fine dining Palestinian restaurant. [00:47-00:50] You walk up, and it looks like someone's home that you're entering. [00:50-00:53] And this was one of the most meaningful dining experiences for us in 2025. [00:53-00:55] Everything in this restaurant speaks of the Palestinian people, [00:55-00:57] their identity and culture. [00:57-00:59] The plates are handcrafted in Palestine. [00:59-01:00] The wine comes from Upper Galilee. [01:00-01:02] It was such a special experience for me to try this wine. [01:02-01:05] Many of the ingredients also come from Palestine, like the olives, [01:05-01:07] and the olive oil used in many of the dishes. [01:07-01:10] My favorite dish was this lamb tenderloin bests-thirma. [01:10-01:13] The lamb is so tender, and the sauce was so frequent, so aromatic. [01:13-01:16] But for me, the most special dish here is their canoffas. [01:16-01:20] It's a canoffa that the owner and chef grew up eating in his hometown of Bethlehem. [01:20-01:22] It's so different from any canoffa I've ever had. [01:22-01:24] And that's why for dessert, I went back to my favorite canoffa shawarma. [01:24-01:26] To try another style of Palestinian canoffa, [01:26-01:29] they make your canoffas fresh every 30 minutes. [01:29-01:32] And they use a very special cheese that is imported from Palestine. [01:32-01:35] It's cheesy, surrepes, so satisfying, especially fresh. [01:35-01:36] Still my favorite canoffa of all time.
